metadata.title:
HAS REVIEW HAD A


CHANGE OF HEART?

pages.raw_text:
Is the Spokesman Review eating crow9


Have the sesults obtained by Nelson W. Durham's
insane fight for a railroad commission developed so
rapidly that the tall tower organ must turn to fawn and
oady to the railroads has roundy abused in order
protection against the conditions brought about
by rabd Durhamism?


The drift of stories anent the traffic mems visit to Spo
kane this morning certainly indicates that last the tall
tower tribe realizes the difficulties into which the obbers
and shippers of Spokane placed by the decduous VIC
tory of Nelson W. Durham in the railroad commission
tangle


In opposing the railroad commission The Press pointed
out the disastrous effects would would nure to the Spo
kane obbers. At that time the Spokesman-Review de-
clared it to be "coarse abuse and villfcation."


But with meticulous truhfulness unusual with that
awful insensae enemy of the railroads. the Spokesman
Review, this morning, says:


with the Inauguration of the new railway commits
sion in this the railway expect crusade by
coast obbers for the annument OF special distributing
rates of Spokane whereby this city protected IN It's
obbing industry for radius of 100 miles The traffic d.
recors expect that the coast towns will call for similar
low rates. which will enable them to invade this field
Should the commission by any chance grant this demand
according the traffic directors the railways will still
protect Spokane by the class rates which are to be
put In effect"


Isn't this exactly what hasbeen argued by The Press
and denied by the Spokesman Review-always with the
execution that until now the tall tower organ did not go
down on it's bones to plead with those awful rail
road magnates to "protect Spokane?


The Spokesman Review worked an injury to Spokane
shippers by rickily putting through railroad commits
sion bill by declaring the railroads to be thieves


Now the mouthpece of Durhamsm is assuring the
shippers that the railroads will protect Spokane from
the railroad commission!


Truly it was time for Traffic Manager Stubs to poke
fun at the Spokane shippers in that truly brilliant talk of
his last night which he closed with the following invita-
ton to ego to Hades'':


1T offensive to in any sense to be up against
the warfare of every day in my fe So can come
to Spokane and listen requests of shippers for
thing they should have according to the best thoughts
of those represent and feel comfortable about it as
I had just granted them everything they asked"


The blame should be placed where it belongs


The Spokesman Review and Durhamism brought
about the railroad commission against the wishes of the
railroads and the Spokane shippers


Now the tall tower tribe, with honeyed peadings is
trying to induce the railroads to "protect' Spokane
